_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning about how places get their names, including the names given by Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples.

_source.w: We are learning about how places get their names, including the names given by Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples.
y: Place names tell us stories about who has lived there and what matters to them.
Learning Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander names helps us respect the oldest knowledge of this land.
Knowing different names for the same place shows us that places hold many stories.
eg: Finding out the Aboriginal name for the river or creek near your school.
Looking at a map and noticing that some streets are named after people or animals.
Asking why your town or suburb has its name and who chose it.
Comparing the Aboriginal name and the English name for the same place.
Making a class chart of interesting place names and what they mean.
Listening to a local Elder share the story behind a place name in your area.
